# Founder Letter

## Why We Exist

We are not building software.

We are reducing the daily stress of running a small business.

Many business owners work harder than everyone else. They wake up early, close
the shop late, manage customers, manage suppliers, manage employees, and still
worry about payroll, taxes, and paperwork.

Software should reduce that burden, not create more of it.

## Our Customers

We build for people who may never have used ERP software. Many have never
studied accounting. Many are running family businesses. Many are working alone.

Our software must respect their time.

## Every Feature Must Answer

- What problem does this solve?
- Would a small business owner use it tomorrow?
- Does it save time?
- Does it reduce stress?

If the answer is no, do not build it.

## Simplicity

Simple is not a lack of features.

Simple is removing unnecessary decisions. Every screen should feel obvious.
Every workflow should feel natural. Every report should tell a story.

## Trust

Business owners trust us with their money, employees, inventory, and taxes.

Never betray that trust. Never fake calculations. Never hide important
information. Never surprise users.

## Tax Assistant

Taxes should never feel scary.

We are not replacing accountants. We help business owners understand what is
happening before they need professional advice.

Knowledge reduces fear.

## Artificial Intelligence

AI exists to explain, not impress.

If AI cannot explain why, it should not recommend.

## Success

Success is not one million downloads.

Success is when a business owner says:

"This software makes my life easier."

If we achieve that, growth will follow.

## Final Principle

Technology changes. Programming languages change. AI changes.

Our mission does not.

We exist to build trusted business assistants that help small businesses
succeed.
